general_info.definition: Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) used as an inductive agent and enhance electrical conductivity component in electrocatalyst for overall water splitting.

method.conditions: Precursors stirred with 5 mL deionized water until completely dissolved. Then heated at 160°C for 4h in Teflon-lined stainless autoclave

structural.crystallinity: lattice fringe of GQDs (002) lattice fringes with a spacing of 0.330nm

applications.performance: As part of G-Mo-Ni3S2/NF composite: HER overpotential = 68 mV at 10 mA/cm-2; OER overpotential = 326 mV at 20 mA/cm-2 in 1.0 M KOH. Full water-splitting cell voltage = 1.58 V at water-splitting current of 10 mA/cm² with stability for more than 50 h.
applications.comparative_advantage: GQDs induce growth of 3D porous hierarchical Ni3S2 nanoflakes, increase active sites and enhance conductivity

feedback.label: Good
